title: ActiveMQ Broker Restart and Persistence Adapter Change
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder file monitor; JMS Messaging Modular Input (Splunkbase 1317) for post-restart client validation
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="activemq:log"
| search "Apache ActiveMQ" AND ("started" OR "Starting" OR "stopped" OR "Stopping" OR "Persistence Adapter" OR "KahaDB")
| table _time, host, _raw
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Broker start/stop banners and persistence adapter swaps flag maintenance windows, failed automatic restarts, or unplanned data-directory changes.
value: Correlates application errors with broker lifecycle events faster than host-level uptime checks alone.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to correlate application errors with broker lifecycle events faster than host-level uptime checks alone.
implementation: Capture both wrapper logs and `activemq.log`. Filter known autoscaler churn via lookup. Alert on stop events outside approved windows.
visualization: Timeline (broker lifecycle), Table (host, message), Single value (restarts per day).
knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
